content_markdown: Which of the following is most likely to increase the rate of global warming?

options.label: B
options.text: Increasing the use of nuclear power stations

options.label: C
options.text: Increasing the use of renewable energy sources

options.label: D
options.text: Using natural gas instead of coal for electricity generation

vibe_explanation: Global warming is primarily driven by the concentration of greenhouse gases like $CO_2$ in the atmosphere. Deforestation increases this rate because trees act as carbon sinks; removing them reduces the amount of $CO_2$ absorbed from the atmosphere and often involves burning or decaying wood which releases stored carbon. Options B, C, and D are all strategies typically used to reduce carbon emissions compared to traditional coal-fired power.
